message:
fix(vsdk): canonicalize ed25519 public keys before comparison to prevent prefix/case mismatches

Public key comparison in the verifier SDKs used raw string equality, which
failed when keys had different representations (e.g., "ed25519:" prefix,
mixed case hex, whitespace). This could cause legitimate signatures to be
rejected or duplicate verifier registrations to slip through.

verifier_sdk.rs:
- Add strip_ed25519_prefix() for explicit prefix handling instead of
  chained trim_start_matches which could strip unrelated characters
- Add canonicalize_ed25519_public_key_hex() to normalize keys to lowercase
  hex before comparison
- migration_artifact_binding_hash() now canonicalizes the signer public key
  before hashing, ensuring binding digests are stable across key formats
- verify_migration_artifact() compares canonicalized keys via constant-time
  comparison instead of raw strings; error messages show canonical forms

universal_verifier_sdk.rs:
- Add validate_capsule_signer_public_key() that parses the signer key as
  an ed25519 verifying key before accepting the manifest
- validate_manifest() now validates the signer key format
- verify_capsule_signature() validates the signer key before attempting
  signature verification
- New tests: test_validate_manifest_malformed_signer_key,
  test_verify_capsule_signature_rejects_malformed_signer_key

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
